title: OpenCode Architecture Reverse Engineering — Confidence Notes

notes.rationale: CLI entry (yargs), Web (SolidJS), Desktop (Tauri) entrypoints clearly separated in packages/. Admin routes well-documented in server/routes/.
Session lifecycle thoroughly documented in architecture.md. session/llm.ts, processor.ts, compaction.ts read in detail. Rotation fallback loop verified against source.
Rotation3D three-dimensional fallback is a key differentiator. ModelVector, FallbackCandidate, FallbackStrategy structures verified from rotation3d.ts. Account resolution chain confirmed from llm.ts.
Tool registry (28 built-in tools), dirty-flag pattern, MCP state machine (available→ready) verified from tool-loader.ts and mcp/app-registry.ts.

workflow-runner.ts confirms RunQueue, Lanes, continuation gates, isPlanTrusting. Governor evaluation (trigger.ts) logic confirmed. Builder quiz (A55) less directly verified — based on architecture.md description + buildBetaAdmissionPrompt function presence.
C gateway (opencode-gateway.c) extensively analyzed. Daemon lifecycle (index.ts) state machine verified: GatewayLock, Signals, Lanes, Drain, Restart. Bus (global.ts) pub/sub confirmed.
All L2 activities (A231-A235, A341-A345, A441-A445, A541-A545, A621-A625) directly mapped to identified functions, exports, and state patterns in source code.

assumptions: Provider list in A31 may grow as new AI SDK providers are added; current list reflects snapshot at analysis time.
MCP transport modes (stdio/SSE/HTTP) coverage may vary per app — specific apps may only support a subset.
Drain timeout (5s) for daemon may be configurable via tweaks.cfg; analyzed as hardcoded default.
Workflow runner Lane capacities (critical:4, normal:3, background:1) may be configurable; analyzed as defaults.
A55 Builder Quiz gate is structurally modeled from architecture.md + function signatures; exact quiz protocol may evolve.
decision_trace: Chose to decompose A23 (not A21/A22/A24) because rotation3d fallback is the most complex subsystem within A2.
Chose to decompose A34 (not A31/A32/A33/A35) because Rotation3D is the core algorithmic differentiator of provider routing.
Chose to decompose A44 (not A41/A42/A43/A45) because MCP app state machine has the richest lifecycle.
Chose to decompose A54 (not A51/A52/A53/A55) because workflow orchestration combines trigger gates, lanes, and drain — crossing multiple concerns.
Chose to decompose A62 (not A61/A63-A66) because per-user daemon has explicit state machine (lock→signal→lanes→drain→restart).
GRAFCET rotation model spans A23 and A34 IDEF0 boundaries to capture the full retry loop faithfully — this is valid per traceability spec (each Step's ModuleRef is valid).
Tool execution GRAFCET uses A42/A43 ModuleRefs reflecting the two-phase pattern: resolve surface (A42) then dispatch (A43).
